projects.title: Driver Behavior Modelling using Model Predictive Control
projects.description: We develop formal safety guarantees for reinforcement learning agents operating in safety-critical environments. Our approach combines Lyapunov-based control barrier functions with deep RL to ensure constraint satisfaction during both training and deployment.

projects.title: Modelling Agressiveness in Driver Behavior using Black Box Simplex Architecture
projects.description: This project models driver aggressiveness using Black-Box Simplex architecture to develop a reactive, behavior-tunable, and safety-aware driver model for autonomous vehicle testing. The ego vehicle is governed by two MPC-based controllers: an advanced controller designed to achieve a target speed, and a baseline safety controller that ensures that the vehicle comes to a complete stop within a specified horizon, enabling effective multi-objective control.

projects.title: Safe Navigation of Multi-robot Sytstem using Runtime Assurance
projects.description: The Multi-Robot BSA System is a multi-agent robotic coordination framework where multiple autonomous robots operate in a shared environment through continuous motion planning, real-time trajectory optimization, and collision avoidance. Each robot dynamically computes feasible paths under environmental constraints while adapting to the motion of other agents.

projects.title: Behavior-Based Traffic Simulation using Model Predictive Control
projects.description: Autonomous Traffic Simulation System is a multi-agent traffic simulation framework where each vehicle independently plans its trajectory in real time using Model Predictive Control (MPC), solving a constrained nonlinear optimization problem at every timestep. Vehicles exhibit realistic driving behaviors including lane-following and overtaking, while actively avoiding collisions with neighboring agents.
